purpose: Current production bundle debt ceiling. The measured v3.51.18 actor-scoped entitlement recovery, history/focus lifecycle and token-minimized iCal revocation surface produces 4,553,784 raw / 1,304,422 gzip JavaScript bytes: +15,980 raw / +4,958 gzip (+0.352% raw) versus the exact reviewed v3.51.17 local artifact. The increase is the localized fail-visible recovery UI, token-free summary/revoke lifecycle, actor-aware entitlement boundary, deterministic retry ownership and accessible deep-link recovery; no dependency was added. The total ceilings retain only the existing 128-byte raw and 279-byte gzip cross-platform/SHA allowances. Largest JavaScript raw rises by the measured 7,985-byte product-contract delta and remains exact; largest gzip retains only the existing 35-byte allowance. CSS is unchanged. Further increases require an explicit reviewed baseline update.
